description_long: This object is a hand tool with a long, slightly curved wooden handle and a large, curved metal blade attached at the top. The handle appears to be made of natural wood, showing visible grain and a light brown color, with two protruding grips for better handling. The blade is wide, single-edged, and has a pronounced curve, resembling a scythe or sickle more than a traditional axe, with a matte metallic finish. The blade is mounted to the handle with a robust metal collar, giving it a secure and sturdy appearance. There are no visible decorative elements, engravings, or branding on the tool. The overall design suggests it is intended for heavy-duty cutting or clearing tasks, possibly for urban landscaping or maintenance.
description: A large, curved-blade hand tool with a wooden handle and metal blade, featuring two grip protrusions and a robust mounting collar. The tool is utilitarian, with a natural wood finish and a matte metal blade.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full curve of the blade and the handle's ergonomic bends and grip protrusions.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the thickness of the handle and the mounting collar, with the blade edge barely visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the blade's curve and the handle's shape, similar to view 0 but mirrored.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the handle's cross-section and the two grip protrusions, with the blade edge not visible.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with visible grain, two perpendicular grip protrusions, oval cross-section, ergonomic and traditional style.
attribute_blade_design: Single, wide, curved steel blade with a sharp edge, no beard or poll, matte finish, functional and utilitarian.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with a metal collar securing the blade to the handle.
